title: Polycarpon involucratum
taxonomy: Polycarpon involucratum is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink pansy or the small-seeded winter-cress. It is native to Europe and Western Asia. This annual or biennial herb is known for its small white or pink flowers and tiny seeds. The plant belongs to the genus Polycarpon, which consists of about six species of flowering plants. In the APG III classification system, it is placed in the order Caryophyllales, which includes other flowering plants like carnations and pinks.
